prompt: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing what revolutionary device in 1876?
Percy Spencer accidentally invented the microwave oven when he noticed his chocolate bar melting near what kind of device?
Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 after returning from a break to find mould had killed bacteria in his petri dish. Where had he been?
options: Recovering from the flu at home
Working night shifts at another hospital

prompt: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at which research organisation?
Georges de Mestral invented Velcro in 1948 after noticing how something kept sticking to his dog's fur during a walk. What was it?
Roy Plunkett accidentally invented Teflon in 1938 at DuPont while searching for what?
options: A new type of engine lubricant

prompt: Walter Hunt invented the safety pin in 1849 to pay off a $15 debt — then sold the patent rights for how much?
Coca-Cola was first sold in 1886 by pharmacist John Pemberton. What specific event directly triggered him to reformulate his wine-based tonic into a non-alcoholic drink?
options: A business partner insisted on removing alcohol
He wanted to market it to children
Atlanta and Fulton County enacted a local alcohol prohibition
A federal law banned cocaine-laced wines

prompt: Spencer Silver accidentally invented the weak, repositionable adhesive behind Post-it notes in 1968 — but it sat unused for years. What was he actually trying to create?
options: A heat-resistant sealant for jet engines
A strong permanent adhesive for aerospace use

prompt: Stephanie Kwolek invented Kevlar at DuPont in 1965. The project that led her there was a search for a lightweight fibre to replace what material in car tyres?
Alfred Nobel's 1867 breakthrough was mixing volatile nitroglycerin with kieselguhr to make it safe to handle. What exactly is kieselguhr?
The claim that Dom Pérignon invented champagne is largely a myth. Who first formally documented adding sugar to wine to trigger a deliberate second fermentation — and in what year?
Thomas Edison is famous for perfecting which everyday device, which he patented in 1879?
The Wright brothers, who built the first successful powered aeroplane, ran what kind of shop before they took to the skies?
Which squishy childhood favourite was originally invented as a cleaning product for wallpaper before being rebranded as a toy?
Johannes Gutenberg's 15th-century invention transformed the world by mass-producing what?
The popsicle was reportedly invented by an 11-year-old who left a cup of soda powder, water, and a stirring stick outside overnight. What froze it solid?
Which of these breakfast foods was invented by the Kellogg brothers, partly as a bland health food meant to discourage certain urges?
The QWERTY keyboard layout we still use today was originally designed for which machine?
Ruth Wakefield, who ran a Massachusetts inn, created the chocolate chip cookie. Famously, she gave Nestlé the rights to print her recipe in exchange for what?
Garrett Morgan, an early-20th-century inventor, patented an improved version of which life-saving device, plus an early traffic signal?
In 1903, decades before it became standard, Mary Anderson patented a hand-operated version of which car accessory after watching a streetcar driver struggle in the snow?
Wilson Greatbatch accidentally invented the implantable cardiac pacemaker when he grabbed the wrong-sized component while building what?
The Frisbee got its name from the Frisbie Pie Company, whose empty tins college students liked to do what with?
Hollywood actress Hedy Lamarr co-invented a 'frequency-hopping' system during WWII that helped lay the groundwork for which modern technology?
Potato chips were supposedly invented by chef George Crum when a customer kept sending back his fried potatoes, complaining they were too what?
WD-40, the famous spray lubricant, got its odd name because it was developed to do what — on the 40th attempt?
Margaret Knight won a patent battle in the 1870s against a man who stole her design for a machine that made what still-used item?
Saccharin, the first artificial sweetener, was discovered in 1879 when chemist Constantin Fahlberg noticed an odd sweetness. Where did he taste it?
options: On his own bread at dinner, from unwashed lab residue
In a spilled beaker of tea

prompt: Charles Goodyear spent years and went into debt perfecting vulcanised rubber. His crucial 1839 breakthrough came when he accidentally did what to a rubber-and-sulphur mixture?
options: Dropped it onto a hot stove

prompt: Percy Shaw invented the 'cat's eye' road reflector in the 1930s. According to the popular account, what inspired the design?
options: The eyes of an actual cat caught in his headlights
Glass beads in a children's toy

prompt: The Super Soaker water gun was invented by Lonnie Johnson, an aerospace engineer, while he was tinkering at home on a project related to what?
